REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (193 Carnelian Way, San Francisco, Ca 94131)
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vincent Yu, Esq., counsel for Thomas Lucas, Trustee (Mr. Yu present in Court); Beth Kershaw, agent for and with David Koeninger, proposed purchaser (Ms. Kershaw present in court, Mr. Koeninger present by video conference); Oliver Burgelman, agent for Trustee, Thomas Lucas (present in court).

The Court notes at the outset the purpose of the hearing: sale of the real property commonly known as 193 Carnelian Way, San Francisco, California. Finding that the petition and related pleadings now appear to be in order, the Court commences sale of the property, reciting the general terms of the sale and the bid price of $1,150,000. The Court recites the first overbid at $1,208,000. After receiving no overbid either in court, over the telephone, or on video conference, the Court confirms the sale at $1,150,000 and authorizes a 5% commission. Bond is set at $1,093,000. Mr. Yu to prepare the order.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy K. Johnson. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello appeared by telephone for Thomas Lucas (Trustee).

The Court expressed concern stating that the supplement seeks additional Attorneys' fees, that it was not served on the other Parties, that the other Parties do not have notice of the fees increasing from $3,500.00 to $9,500.00, and questions if the conservatorship assessment fee was paid twice (January 26, 2018 and March 18, 2018). Ms. Robello stated that she believes the second supplement only addresses the issue of a filing fee that was reduced from $450.00 to $200.00, that she does not have Examiner's notes about the Attorneys' fees and that she only has notes about the reduction of costs. The Court noted that in the initial petition the fee on paragraph 5 of the prayer is $3,500.00 and that in the second supplement of June 10th the fee is $9,597.00. Ms. Robello stated that she believes there is a typographical error, that the attached exhibit in the initial petition matches the $9,597.00 fee amount and is the correct fee, and that it looks like the prayer on the initial petition was incorrect. Ms. Robello also stated that she is fine with the Court removing $800.00 from the fee request. The Court noted that exact amount of fees in the amount of $9,597.00 is laid out in the initial petition. The Court granted the Fourth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.
